<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#state-and-action-space-contract" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#state-and-action-space-contract">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq4-planning" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq4-planning">Master Thesis Research Questions</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-oracle-rollout-state">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Oracle Rollout State</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">oracle state</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.state</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>oracle state</code> <code>privileged rollout state</code> <code>GT rollout state</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$s_t^{\mathrm{oracle}}$</span><code>rl.s_oracle</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$s_t^{\mathrm{oracle}}=(s_t^{\mathrm{cf+}},\mathcal{M}^{\mathrm{GT}},\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}},\{D_{t,i}^{\mathrm{GT}},\mathcal{P}_{t,i}^{\mathrm{GT}},\mathrm{RRI}_{t,i}\}_{i=1}^{N_t})$</span><code>rl.s_oracle</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Privileged rollout state for labels, upper bounds, and evaluation.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The oracle rollout state may contain GT mesh geometry, GT target crops, GT OBBs, all-candidate synthetic depth and point clouds, derived normals, mesh-face visibility, Chamfer/RRI terms, and oracle scores. These fields support label generation and diagnostics but are not actor-visible inputs for the main scorer or Q_H model.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-ground-truth-target-evaluation">GT-EVAL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oracle-rri">oracle RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-specific-rri">target RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-minimal-counterfactual-state">CF0 state</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#state-and-action-space-contract" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#state-and-action-space-contract">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rri_theory.html" title="docs/contents/theory/rri_theory.qmd">Relative Reconstruction Improvement (RRI) Theory</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-finite-candidate-action-set">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Finite Candidate Action Set</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">action set</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.mdp</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>candidate action set</code> <code>masked candidate set</code> <code>finite NBV action space</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{A}(s_t)$</span><code>rl.action_set</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$q_{t,i}$</span><code>oracle.candidate_qti</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{Q}_t$</span><code>oracle.candidates_t</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$m_{t,i}$</span><code>rl.validity_mask</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{Q}_t=\{q_{t,i}\}_{i=1}^{N_t},\quad \mathcal{A}(s_t)=\{i\in\{1,\ldots,N_t\}:m_{t,i}=1\},\quad q_t=q_{t,a_t}$</span><code>rl.finite_action_set</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Masked finite action-index set over sampled candidate views.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">At each rollout step, ARIA-NBV samples a finite candidate table Q_t={q_{t,i}}. The admissible action set contains indices i whose validity mask m_{t,i} is true, and selecting a_t chooses pose q_t=q_{t,a_t}. This keeps planning bounded and preserves invalidity as a feasibility constraint rather than a low-quality RRI label.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Masked finite action-index set**

$$
\mathcal{Q}_t=\{q_{t,i}\}_{i=1}^{N_t},\quad \mathcal{A}(s_t)=\{i\in\{1,\ldots,N_t\}:m_{t,i}=1\},\quad q_t=q_{t,a_t}
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-validity-mask">mask</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-rollout-state">state</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq3-candidates" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq3-candidates">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#rl-planning-theory" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#rl-planning-theory">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateViewGenerator.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateViewGenerator.qmd">CandidateViewGenerator</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-counterfactual-transition">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Counterfactual Transition</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">transition</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.mdp</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>rollout transition</code> <code>candidate fusion update</code> <code>counterfactual update</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$T$</span><code>rl.transition</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{P}$</span><code>oracle.points</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{P}_q$</span><code>oracle.points_q</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{P}_{t+1}=\mathcal{P}_t\cup\mathcal{P}_{q_t}$</span><code>rl.counterfactual_transition</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Replayable state update after selecting a candidate index.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">For the thesis-core ASE mesh/oracle loop, the transition uses the selected candidate index to render or retrieve that candidate's depth, backproject points, and update the counterfactual point state and selected-view history. All-candidate GT renders and scores remain oracle-only before selection. The update must be deterministic under the stored seed and lineage.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Point-state transition**

$$
\mathcal{P}_{t+1}=\mathcal{P}_t\cup\mathcal{P}_{q_t}
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-rollout-state">state</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oracle-rri">oracle RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#rl-planning-theory" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#rl-planning-theory">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq4-planning" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq4-planning">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CounterfactualPoseGenerator.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CounterfactualPoseGenerator.qmd">CounterfactualPoseGenerator</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-target-rri-reward">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Target-RRI Reward</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">reward</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">metrics.reconstruction_quality</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>target reward</code> <code>quality reward</code> <code>target-specific RRI reward</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$r_t^e$</span><code>rl.reward_target</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathrm{RRI}_e$</span><code>entity.rri_e</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{P}$</span><code>oracle.points</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}}$</span><code>ase.mesh_target</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$r_t^e=\mathrm{RRI}_e(q_t\mid \mathcal{P}_t,\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}})$</span><code>rl.target_rri_reward</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Quality-only immediate reward equal to target-specific RRI for the selected candidate.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The main thesis reward is cumulative target RRI under equal acquisition budget. Log-improvement and episode-normalized variants remain visible follow-up reward ablations, not the default target for the first Q_H result.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Target-RRI reward**

$$
r_t^e=\mathrm{RRI}_e(q_t\mid \mathcal{P}_t,\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}})
$$
:::

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Log-improvement follow-up**

$$
r_t^{\log,e}=\log(D(\mathcal{P}_t^e,\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}})+\varepsilon)-\log(D(\mathcal{P}_{t+1}^e,\mathcal{M}_e^{\mathrm{GT}})+\varepsilon)
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-specific-rri">target RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-finite-horizon-return">return</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-acquisition-cost">cost</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq1-objective" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq1-objective">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#rl-planning-theory" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#rl-planning-theory">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/roadmap.html#roadmap-m5" title="docs/contents/thesis/roadmap.qmd#roadmap-m5">Master Thesis Roadmap</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-VIN-NBV-frahm2025">&#64;VIN-NBV-frahm2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-finite-horizon-return">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Finite-Horizon Return</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">return</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.objective</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>H-step return</code> <code>bounded return</code> <code>cumulative target RRI</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$G_t^{(H)}$</span><code>rl.return_h</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$r_t^e$</span><code>rl.reward_target</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\gamma$</span><code>rl.gamma</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$H$</span><code>rl.H</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$G_t^{(H)}=\sum_{k=0}^{H-1}\gamma^k r_{t+k}^e$</span><code>rl.finite_horizon_return</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">H-step discounted return over target-RRI rewards.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The return definition keeps gamma symbolic so discounted ablations remain possible. The first thesis result should report cumulative target RRI under an equal acquisition budget and treat log-improvement or normalized rewards as follow-up analysis.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Finite-horizon return**

$$
G_t^{(H)}=\sum_{k=0}^{H-1}\gamma^k r_{t+k}^e
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-rri-reward">reward</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-finite-horizon-q-function">Q_H</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#rl-planning-theory" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#rl-planning-theory">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq4-planning" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq4-planning">Master Thesis Research Questions</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-finite-horizon-q-function">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Finite-Horizon Q Function</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">Q_H</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">model.value</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>Q_H</code> <code>candidate-query Q_H</code> <code>bounded Q function</code> <code>finite-candidate Q</code> <code>fitted Double-Q head</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$Q_H$</span><code>rl.qh</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$G_t^{(H)}$</span><code>rl.return_h</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$s_t^{\mathrm{cf0}}$</span><code>rl.s_cf0</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$a$</span><code>rl.a</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$Q_H(s_t^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_t)=\mathbb{E}\left[G_t^{(H)}\mid s_t=s_t^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_t\right]$</span><code>rl.q_h</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$y_t^Q=r_t+\gamma V(s_{t+1})$</span><code>rl.q_backup</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Finite-horizon candidate-value function for target-conditioned ARIA-NBV.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The mandatory M5 learned policy-like result is Q_H over finite candidate sets. The first-path architecture is a candidate-query Transformer: encode s_t^{cf0}, actor-visible target descriptor z_e, selected-view history, budget state, and candidate tokens, then emit one value per candidate. DQN contributes replayed transition learning and Bellman-style finite-action value targets; Double DQN contributes the masked online-selector / target-evaluator backup to reduce max-over-candidate overestimation; IQL contributes the offline support rule that value learning must not query invalid, ungenerated, or unavailable actions. Q_H must respect validity masks and beat one-step greedy or model scoring on cumulative target RRI under equal acquisition budget, with bounded oracle lookahead as an upper bound.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Finite-horizon candidate value**

$$
Q_H(s_t^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_t,z_e)=\mathbb{E}\left[G_t^{(H)}\mid s_t=s_t^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_t,z_e\right]
$$
:::

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Masked Double-DQN selector**

$$
j^*=\arg\max_{j:m_{t+1,j}=1}Q_\theta(s_{t+1}^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_{t+1,j},z_e)
$$
:::

::: {.glossary-formula}
**Masked Double-DQN target**

$$
y_t=r_t^e+\gamma(1-d_t)Q_{\bar\theta}(s_{t+1}^{\mathrm{cf0}},a_{t+1,j^*},z_e)
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-finite-horizon-return">return</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-minimal-counterfactual-state">CF0 state</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-predicted-target-q">PRED-Q</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-validity-mask">mask</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq4-planning" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq4-planning">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/roadmap.html#roadmap-m5" title="docs/contents/thesis/roadmap.qmd#roadmap-m5">Master Thesis Roadmap</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rl_planning.html#q-h-training-contract" title="docs/contents/theory/rl_planning.qmd#q-h-training-contract">Finite-Candidate Rollout And Q_H Contract</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/rl_planning.html#q-h-and-dqn" title="docs/contents/literature/rl_planning.qmd#q-h-and-dqn">RL Sources For Rollout And Q_H</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-DBLP:journals/corr/MnihKSGAWR13">&#64;DBLP:journals/corr/MnihKSGAWR13</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-DoubleDQN-vanHasselt2015">&#64;DoubleDQN-vanHasselt2015</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-IQL-kostrikov2021">&#64;IQL-kostrikov2021</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-validity-mask">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Validity Mask</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">mask</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.constraints</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>candidate validity mask</code> <code>action mask</code> <code>invalid action mask</code>
</div>

<div class="glossary-card-notation">
<div><strong>Symbols</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-symbols"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$m_{t,i}$</span><code>rl.validity_mask</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\rho_{t,i}$</span><code>rl.invalid_reason</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$m$</span><code>vin.cand_valid</code></span></div></div>
<div><strong>Equations</strong><div class="glossary-notation-list glossary-notation-equations"><span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$m_i=\mathbb{1}[\mathrm{finite}]\mathbb{1}[v_i>0]\mathbb{1}[v_i^{\mathrm{sem}}>0]$</span><code>metrics.candidate_validity</code></span> <span class="glossary-notation-item"><span class="glossary-notation-math">$\mathcal{Q}_t=\{q_{t,i}\}_{i=1}^{N_t},\quad \mathcal{A}(s_t)=\{i\in\{1,\ldots,N_t\}:m_{t,i}=1\},\quad q_t=q_{t,a_t}$</span><code>rl.finite_action_set</code></span></div></div>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Hard mask that separates feasible candidate actions from invalid candidates.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The mask m_{t,i} gates candidate actions, while invalid reason codes rho_{t,i} preserve why a candidate was rejected. Collision, outside-bounds poses, no target visibility, bad frusta, no depth hits, and outside-EVL-extent cases are constraints rather than low target-RRI examples.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-finite-candidate-action-set">action set</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-finite-horizon-q-function">Q_H</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-rri-reward">reward</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq-evidence-protocol" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq-evidence-protocol">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateSamplingResult.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.pose_generation.CandidateSamplingResult.qmd">CandidateSamplingResult</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-core" id="term-project-aria">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Project Aria</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-core">core</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.project_aria</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>Aria</code> <code>Project Aria ecosystem</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Egocentric research-device and tooling ecosystem for calibrated, time-aligned multimodal sensing.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV treats Project Aria and its MPS-style products as the actor-visible sensing contract, while ASE meshes remain offline supervision and evaluation assets.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-machine-perception-services">MPS</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/project_aria.html" title="docs/contents/literature/project_aria.qmd">Project Aria</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-projectaria-engel2023">&#64;projectaria-engel2023</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Dataset {#glossary-category-dataset}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-aria-digital-twin">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Aria Digital Twin</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">ADT</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.aria</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>ADT</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Project Aria dataset with real-world captures and digital-twin scene annotations.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ADT is adjacent to ARIA-NBV's sim-to-real context; the current experiments focus on ASE and EFM3D/ATEK exports, while ADT remains a relevant transfer surface.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-everyday-objects">AEO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-aria-everyday-objects">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Aria Everyday Objects</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">AEO</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.aria</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>AEO</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Small-scale real-world Project Aria object dataset used by EFM3D for egocentric 3D perception evaluation.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">AEO is relevant as a possible sim-to-real check for ARIA-NBV ideas that are first developed on ASE mesh-supervised snippets.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-digital-twin">ADT</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-virtual-reality-standard">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Virtual Reality Standard</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">VRS</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.file_format</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>VRS file</code> <code>Project Aria VRS</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">File format used by Project Aria tooling to store multi-modal sensor recordings efficiently.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">VRS is part of the upstream Project Aria ecosystem, while ARIA-NBV primarily consumes ASE/ATEK-derived tensorized snippets for current experiments.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-snippet">snippet</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-multi-frame-camera-data">MFCD</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-motion-trajectory-data">MTD</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="setup.html" title="docs/contents/setup.qmd">Setup Instructions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-central-pupil-frame">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Central Pupil Frame</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">CPF</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.frames</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>central pupil coordinate frame</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Coordinate frame placed at the midpoint between the left and right eye boxes of Project Aria glasses.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">CPF is used by Project Aria for gaze-related quantities and should remain distinct from rig, camera, world, and PyTorch3D frames in ARIA-NBV docs.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-left-up-forward">LUF</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-machine-perception-services">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Machine Perception Services</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">MPS</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.project_aria</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>Project Aria MPS</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Project Aria processing services that derive pose, mapping, gaze, hand, and related perception outputs from sensor recordings.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV mainly depends on MPS-style pose and semi-dense mapping products as the current reconstruction state for RRI computation.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-simultaneous-localization-and-mapping">SLAM</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/semi-dense-pc.html" title="docs/contents/theory/semi-dense-pc.qmd">Semi-Dense Point Clouds</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-snippet">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Snippet</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">snippet</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.sample</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>temporal window</code> <code>ASE snippet</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Short synchronized temporal window of Aria sensor data used as one EVL/VIN input sample.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">A snippet typically contains RGB or grayscale streams, poses, calibration, semi-dense points, and scene metadata that EVL lifts into a voxel grid.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-view-introspection-network">VIN</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/data_handling.efm_dataset.html" title="docs/reference/data_handling.efm_dataset.qmd">efm_dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-scene-script-language">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">SceneScript Language</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">SSL</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.scene_representation</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>Structure Scene Language</code> <code>SceneScript structured language</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Structured language representation for indoor scene layout using primitives such as walls, doors, windows, and objects.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">SceneScript is relevant to ARIA-NBV as a possible semantic/global planning layer and as one source of ASE scene-structure context.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-of-interest">target</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-next-best-view">NBV</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/scene_script.html" title="docs/contents/literature/scene_script.qmd">SceneScript</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-SceneScript-avetisyan2024">&#64;SceneScript-avetisyan2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-motion-trajectory-data">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Motion Trajectory Data</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">MTD</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.stream</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>trajectory data</code> <code>pose stream</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Device poses over time, usually represented as a sequence of 6-DoF transformations.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">MTD supplies the logged egocentric trajectory used to define snippet state, current reconstruction context, and candidate-view reference poses.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-snippet">snippet</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/data_handling.efm_views.html" title="docs/reference/data_handling.efm_views.qmd">efm_views</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-multi-frame-camera-data">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Multi-Frame Camera Data</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">MFCD</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.stream</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>multi-camera frame data</code> <code>synchronized camera data</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Synchronized camera streams from multiple Project Aria cameras over a temporal window.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">MFCD provides the egocentric image evidence consumed by EVL and aligned with pose, calibration, and semi-dense point streams.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-snippet">snippet</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/efm3d.html" title="docs/contents/literature/efm3d.qmd">EFM3D and EVL</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-multi-semi-dense-point-data">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Multi-Semi-Dense Point Data</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">MSDPD</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.stream</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>multi-frame semi-dense points</code> <code>semi-dense point stream</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Semi-dense 3D point observations generated by SLAM-style processing across a snippet or trajectory window.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">MSDPD is the sparse observed geometry that ARIA-NBV fuses with candidate point clouds and compares against ground-truth meshes for RRI labels.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-simultaneous-localization-and-mapping">SLAM</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/semi-dense-pc.html" title="docs/contents/theory/semi-dense-pc.qmd">Semi-Dense Point Clouds</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rri_theory.html" title="docs/contents/theory/rri_theory.qmd">Relative Reconstruction Improvement (RRI) Theory</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-aria-synthetic-environments">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Aria Synthetic Environments</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">ASE</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">dataset.synthetic</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>Aria Synthetic Environment</code> <code>ASE dataset</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Large-scale synthetic indoor dataset with simulated Project Aria sensor characteristics, egocentric trajectories, and scene annotations.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV uses ASE snippets and the public mesh-supervised subset to generate oracle RRI labels from ground-truth meshes and semi-dense point clouds.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-snippet">snippet</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-foundation-model-3d">EFM3D</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oracle-rri">oracle RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="setup.html#aria-synthetic-environments" title="docs/contents/setup.qmd#aria-synthetic-environments">Setup Instructions</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Geometry {#glossary-category-geometry}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-oriented-bounding-box">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Oriented Bounding Box</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">OBB</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">geometry.annotation</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>oriented box</code> <code>object box</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">3D bounding box with arbitrary orientation, used to represent object extent more tightly than an axis-aligned box.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">OBBs are a natural target encoding for entity-aware ARIA-NBV because they provide center, extent, orientation, semantic class, and confidence signals.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-of-interest">target</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-conditioned-scorer">target-conditioned scorer</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html#oriented-bounding-boxes" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd#oriented-bounding-boxes">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/questions.html#rq2-target-encoding" title="docs/contents/thesis/questions.qmd#rq2-target-encoding">Master Thesis Research Questions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/vin.types.html" title="docs/reference/vin.types.qmd">types</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-frustum">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Frustum</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">frustum</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">geometry.camera</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>viewing frustum</code> <code>camera frustum</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Truncated pyramidal camera-visible volume bounded by near and far clipping planes plus lateral field-of-view planes.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Candidate-view frusta define which scene surfaces can project into a camera image and are therefore central to visibility, rendering, and RRI diagnostics.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rri_theory.html" title="docs/contents/theory/rri_theory.qmd">Relative Reconstruction Improvement (RRI) Theory</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-Frustum-Wikipedia-2025">&#64;Frustum-Wikipedia-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-left-up-forward">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Left-Up-Forward</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">LUF</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">geometry.frames</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>LUF frame</code> <code>left up forward</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Camera coordinate convention whose x axis points left, y axis points up, and z axis points forward.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">LUF is one of the coordinate-frame conventions that must stay explicit when moving between Project Aria cameras, PyTorch3D cameras, and ARIA-NBV candidate poses.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-frustum">frustum</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ">05 Coordinate Conventions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ">12F Appendix Pose Frames</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-degrees-of-freedom">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Degrees of Freedom</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">DoF</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">geometry.pose</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>DoF</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Number of independent pose parameters available to a camera, object, or action representation.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV distinguishes full 6-DoF pose estimation from reduced 5-DoF candidate or action spaces used for practical view planning.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ">05 Coordinate Conventions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ">12F Appendix Pose Frames</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-six-degrees-of-freedom">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Six Degrees of Freedom</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">6DoF</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">geometry.pose</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>6-DoF</code> <code>6 degrees of freedom</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Pose parameterization with three translational and three rotational degrees of freedom.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Project Aria poses, candidate cameras, and world-to-device transforms are usually represented as 6-DoF rigid-body transforms.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-degrees-of-freedom">DoF</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/05-coordinate-conventions.typ">05 Coordinate Conventions</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/12f-appendix-pose-frames.typ">12F Appendix Pose Frames</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Metrics {#glossary-category-metrics}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-coverage-ratio">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Coverage Ratio</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">CR</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">metrics.coverage</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>coverage ratio</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Fraction of a target surface, scene, or region treated as observed under a chosen visibility or distance threshold.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Coverage ratio is a useful diagnostic baseline for NBV, but ARIA-NBV treats reconstruction-quality improvement through RRI as the preferred optimization target.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/nbv_background.html" title="docs/contents/theory/nbv_background.qmd">NBV Background</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rri_theory.html" title="docs/contents/theory/rri_theory.qmd">Relative Reconstruction Improvement (RRI) Theory</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-VIN-NBV-frahm2025">&#64;VIN-NBV-frahm2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-area-under-curve">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Area Under Curve</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">AUC</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">metrics.evaluation</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>AUC</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Aggregate score computed by integrating a metric curve over an acquisition, threshold, or ranking axis.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">AUC can summarize how quickly reconstruction quality, coverage, or ranking quality improves as views are acquired or candidate thresholds change.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-acquisition-cost">cost</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/nbv_background.html" title="docs/contents/theory/nbv_background.qmd">NBV Background</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-ground-truth">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Ground Truth</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">GT</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">metrics.reference</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>reference annotation</code> <code>oracle reference</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Reference data or annotations treated as the trusted target for training, validation, or evaluation.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">In ARIA-NBV, ground truth usually refers to ASE meshes, object annotations, poses, or labels used to compute oracle supervision and diagnostic metrics.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oracle-rri">oracle RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oriented-bounding-box">OBB</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/rri_theory.html" title="docs/contents/theory/rri_theory.qmd">Relative Reconstruction Improvement (RRI) Theory</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Model {#glossary-category-model}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-egocentric-foundation-model-3d">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Egocentric Foundation Model 3D</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">EFM3D</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">model.backbone</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>EFM</code> <code>egocentric foundation model</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Egocentric 3D foundation-model stack used as the frozen spatial backbone for ARIA-NBV candidate scoring.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">The current project uses EFM3D and its EVL architecture to expose voxel occupancy, centerness, semantic, and OBB evidence for VIN-style RRI prediction.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-view-introspection-network">VIN</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-aria-synthetic-environments">ASE</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/efm3d.html" title="docs/contents/literature/efm3d.qmd">EFM3D and EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.backbone_evl.EvlBackbone.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.backbone_evl.EvlBackbone.qmd">EvlBackbone</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Egocentric Voxel Lifting</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">EVL</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">model.backbone</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>voxel lifting</code> <code>EVL backbone</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">EFM3D architecture that lifts synchronized egocentric observations into a gravity-aligned 3D voxel feature volume.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV uses EVL head outputs and voxel evidence as the frozen scene representation for VIN-style RRI prediction.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-foundation-model-3d">EFM3D</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-view-introspection-network">VIN</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-oriented-bounding-box">OBB</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/efm3d.html" title="docs/contents/literature/efm3d.qmd">EFM3D and EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.backbone_evl.EvlBackbone.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.backbone_evl.EvlBackbone.qmd">EvlBackbone</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a> <a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EVL-Doc-2025">&#64;EVL-Doc-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-view-introspection-network">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">View Introspection Network</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">VIN</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">model.scoring</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>VIN scorer</code> <code>VIN-NBV model</code> <code>Aria-VIN-NBV</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Learned candidate-view scorer that predicts RRI or an ordinal RRI-derived utility without capturing the candidate view.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV adapts VIN-NBV by placing a lightweight RRI prediction head on top of frozen EVL features and candidate-pose evidence from ASE snippets.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-target-conditioned-scorer">target-conditioned scorer</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/vin_nbv.html" title="docs/contents/literature/vin_nbv.qmd">VIN-NBV</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/vin.model_v3.html" title="docs/reference/vin.model_v3.qmd">model_v3</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-VIN-NBV-frahm2025">&#64;VIN-NBV-frahm2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Planning {#glossary-category-planning}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-five-degrees-of-freedom">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Five Degrees of Freedom</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">5DoF</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">planning.action_space</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>5-DoF</code> <code>5 degrees of freedom</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Reduced camera-action parameterization commonly used when roll is fixed or otherwise constrained.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV uses 5-DoF language for candidate and planning abstractions where position and viewing direction matter while roll is not an independently optimized control dimension.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-candidate-view">candidate</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-next-best-view">NBV</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-degrees-of-freedom">DoF</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="thesis/roadmap.html" title="docs/contents/thesis/roadmap.qmd">Master Thesis Roadmap</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/10a-extensions.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/10a-extensions.typ">10A Extensions</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-GenNBV-chen2024">&#64;GenNBV-chen2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Reconstruction {#glossary-category-reconstruction}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-track">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Track</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">track</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">reconstruction.features</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>feature track</code> <code>2D track</code> <code>SLAM track</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Temporal sequence of corresponding image-feature detections across frames, usually carrying per-frame image coordinates, timestamps, and camera IDs.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Tracks can be triangulated or optimized into 3D points, making them a bridge between image evidence and the semi-dense point clouds used by ARIA-NBV.</p>

::: {.glossary-formula}
$$
\mathcal{T}=\{(u_k,v_k,\mathrm{cam}_k,t_k)\}_{k=0}^{N}
$$
:::

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-simultaneous-localization-and-mapping">SLAM</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-visual-inertial-odometry">VIO</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/semi-dense-pc.html" title="docs/contents/theory/semi-dense-pc.qmd">Semi-Dense Point Clouds</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-multi-view-stereo">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Multi-view Stereo</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">MVS</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">reconstruction.geometry</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>multi-view reconstruction</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Reconstruction family that estimates dense or semi-dense scene geometry from multiple calibrated views.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">MVS is part of the broader reconstruction context for NBV, although ARIA-NBV's current oracle labels are built from ASE meshes and Aria-style semi-dense point observations.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-next-best-view">NBV</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/nbv_background.html" title="docs/contents/theory/nbv_background.qmd">NBV Background</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-visual-inertial-odometry">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Visual-Inertial Odometry</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">VIO</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">reconstruction.pose</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>visual inertial odometry</code> <code>inertial visual odometry</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Pose-estimation method that combines visual measurements from cameras with inertial measurements from IMUs.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Project Aria pose and mapping products build on visual-inertial estimation, and ARIA-NBV depends on those pose streams for snippet state and candidate frame contracts.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-simultaneous-localization-and-mapping">SLAM</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-motion-trajectory-data">MTD</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/semi-dense-pc.html" title="docs/contents/theory/semi-dense-pc.qmd">Semi-Dense Point Clouds</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-simultaneous-localization-and-mapping">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Simultaneous Localization and Mapping</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">SLAM</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">reconstruction.state</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>visual SLAM</code> <code>mapping and localization</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Method for estimating sensor motion while building a map of the surrounding scene.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">In ARIA-NBV, logged SLAM poses and semi-dense points form the current reconstruction state against which candidate-view RRI is evaluated.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-point-cloud">PC</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-snippet">snippet</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="theory/semi-dense-pc.html" title="docs/contents/theory/semi-dense-pc.qmd">Semi-Dense Point Clouds</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="ase_dataset.html" title="docs/contents/ase_dataset.qmd">Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) Dataset</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-ProjectAria-ASE-2025">&#64;ProjectAria-ASE-2025</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

## Representation {#glossary-category-representation}

<div class="glossary-card-grid">

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-background" id="term-three-dimensional-gaussian-splatting">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">3D Gaussian Splatting</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-badge-short">3DGS</span>
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-background">background</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">representation.radiance_field</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>3D Gaussian Splatting</code> <code>3DGS</code> <code>Gaussian splatting</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Explicit radiance-field representation using optimized 3D Gaussian primitives for real-time novel-view synthesis.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">ARIA-NBV treats active 3DGS work as proposal, uncertainty, and simulator-bridge literature, not as a replacement for ASE mesh-supervised RRI.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-next-best-view">NBV</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-relative-reconstruction-improvement">RRI</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="literature/active_3dgs_nbv.html" title="docs/contents/literature/active_3dgs_nbv.qmd">Active 3DGS and Targeted NBV</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-GaussianSplatting-kerbl2023">&#64;GaussianSplatting-kerbl2023</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

<article class="glossary-card glossary-tier-support" id="term-occupancy-grid">
<div class="glossary-card-header">
<div class="glossary-term-title">Occupancy Grid</div>
<div class="glossary-term-badges">
<span class="glossary-badge glossary-tier-badge glossary-tier-badge-support">support</span>
<span class="glossary-badge">representation.spatial</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="glossary-aliases">
<span>Aliases</span>
<code>occupancy volume</code>
</div>

<p class="glossary-definition">Spatial grid whose cells encode whether space is occupied, free, unknown, or represented by a related occupancy probability.</p>

<p class="glossary-detail-text">Occupancy-style voxel evidence appears in EVL outputs and VIN feature construction, where it helps summarize local 3D scene state for candidate scoring.</p>

<details class="glossary-details">
<summary>Links and references</summary>
<div class="glossary-metadata">
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Related</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-egocentric-voxel-lifting">EVL</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="#term-view-introspection-network">VIN</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>Docs</span><div><a class="glossary-chip" href="../reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.html" title="docs/reference/aria_nbv.vin.model_v3.VinModelV3.qmd">VinModelV3</a> <a class="glossary-chip" href="../typst/seminar_paper/sections/06-architecture.typ" title="docs/typst/seminar_paper/sections/06-architecture.typ">06 Architecture</a></div></div>
<div class="glossary-metadata-row"><span>References</span><div><a class="glossary-chip glossary-citation" href="#ref-EFM3D-straub2024">&#64;EFM3D-straub2024</a></div></div>
</div>
</details>

</article>

</div>

::: {.glossary-citation-seed}
[@DBLP:journals/corr/MnihKSGAWR13]; [@DoubleDQN-vanHasselt2015]; [@EFM3D-straub2024]; [@EVL-Doc-2025]; [@Frustum-Wikipedia-2025]; [@GaussianSplatting-kerbl2023]; [@GenNBV-chen2024]; [@IQL-kostrikov2021]; [@ProjectAria-ASE-2025]; [@SceneScript-avetisyan2024]; [@VIN-NBV-frahm2025]; [@projectaria-engel2023]
:::

:::
